title: galanthus nivalis subsp. reflexus
intro: Galanthus nivalis subsp. reflexus, commonly known as the Snowdrop, is a subspecies of flowering plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. It is known for its white, bell-shaped flowers that bloom in early spring, often through snow, providing a welcome sight in the winter landscape. The Snowdrop has been used in traditional medicine for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can help to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Galanthus nivalis subsp. reflexus can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and have been used to promote digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
